title: pluchea senecioides
intro: Pluchea senecioides, also known as White Buphthalmum or Wild Camphor, is a plant species that belongs to the family Asteraceae. It is traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant properties. The health benefits of Pluchea senecioides include its ability to relieve respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its use in treating wounds and skin conditions. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and roots are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
